name: tools/validate.sh Repo-Local Compliance Validator + NIZAM.json Indexing

files_create.purpose:
The repo-local NDS compliance validator (docs/architecture/ADR-001-ci-compliance-enforcement.md Decision; product_spec_002.md F-012). Bash script, `#!/usr/bin/env bash` shebang, `set -euo pipefail` as its first executable line, mode 0755 (executable). One runtime-agnostic command: `bash tools/validate.sh` exits 0 on a clean tree and non-zero on any violation. Runtime dependencies: bash, grep, find, git, python3 (with the `jsonschema` and `yaml` (PyYAML) modules importable). MUST fail closed with a clear, named diagnostic if any dependency is missing or a required python3 module fails to import -- mirror bootstrap.sh's `require_command()` pattern (repo root `bootstrap.sh`, function at approximately line 158: `command -v "${cmd}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || die ...`) with an equivalent `require_command` helper for the shell tools, plus a `require_python_module` helper (invoking `python3 -c 'import jsonschema'` / `python3 -c 'import yaml'` and failing with a named diagnostic identifying exactly which module is missing) run before any check executes. NOTE: `bootstrap.sh` lives at the REPO ROOT (`./bootstrap.sh`), not under `tools/` -- do not reference a nonexistent `tools/bootstrap.sh` path anywhere in this script or its --help text.

Modes: (1) default (no args) -- runs the full repo sweep, all checks C1-C8, prints one PASS/FAIL line per check function plus a final summary line and exits 0 only if every check passed. (2) `--help` -- prints usage documenting every check (C1-C8), both modes, the runtime dependencies, and explicitly documents that C8 compares the working tree's frontmatter `version` against the `git show HEAD:<file>` revision of the same file (so C8 is only meaningful in CI on a PR diff against `HEAD`; on a repo with no prior HEAD version of a file, C8 treats the file as new and skips the version-diff comparison for it, requiring no change record) -- then exits 0. (3) `--target <file>` -- runs only the checks applicable to a single file (frontmatter/format checks C1/C2 and the fence check C3 for a `.md` target; the NIZAM.json schema+walker check C4 for a `.json` target matching that shape) against exactly the one named file, printing PASS/FAIL per applicable check and exiting non-zero if any applicable check fails. `--target` is what drives the three negative fixtures below; it is the only mode under which fixture files are ever read.

CONTRACTED OUTPUT FORMAT (load-bearing, asserted by verification below -- not merely descriptive): validate.sh operates on the repository rooted at the current working directory at invocation time (documented explicitly in --help), so `cd <any-repo-copy> && bash tools/validate.sh` (relative invocation) always evaluates that copy's own tree, never a hardcoded absolute path back to this original checkout -- this is what makes the script usable against mutated clones for isolated behavioral testing. Each of the 8 check functions, when it runs, emits EXACTLY one line matching `^\[C<N>\] (PASS|FAIL)( .*)?$` (e.g. `[C5] PASS branding-leakage` or `[C5] FAIL branding-leakage: nizamiq found in CONTEXT.md:42`) -- the tag and PASS/FAIL keyword are the load-bearing, grep-stable prefix; the trailing check name and detail are free text. The last line of stdout in every invocation (default, --target, or --help) is always exactly `SUMMARY: <passed> passed, <failed> failed`, where `<passed>` + `<failed>` equals the number of checks that actually ran (always 8 in default mode; only the applicable subset under --target). Exit code is 0 only when `<failed>` is 0.

Checks (each implemented as a separate named bash function per the output format above, each failure additionally printing the specific offending file(s)/path(s) on the line or lines following its `[C<N>] FAIL` line):
- C1 (frontmatter schema): every shipped `.md` file -- `CONTEXT.md`; every `.md` under `standard/`, `methodology/`, `registry/`, `templates/`, `tools/` (EXCLUDING `tools/fixtures/`, which is never part of the default/no-args sweep); `schema/README.md` (the only `.md` under `schema/`); every `.md` under `docs/architecture/` -- begins with a YAML frontmatter block (first line exactly `---`, a second `---` closing it) that schema-validates via `python3` + `jsonschema` against `schema/frontmatter.schema.json`. Root `README.md` and `docs/planning/*` are NOT part of this sweep (no frontmatter contract on them).
- C2 (format, belt-and-braces beyond schema): for the same file set as C1, `authoritative_source` equals the file's own repository-relative path exactly, or the literal string `NA`; `status` is one of `draft`/`active`/`deprecated` (redundant with the schema enum, asserted again explicitly per the contract's own belt-and-braces requirement); `version` matches semver `MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH` (redundant with the schema pattern, asserted again explicitly).
- C3 (untagged fence sweep, NDS Sec 6.2): the awk fence-parity scan already proven in this repo's own QA history (contracts 009/010's verification command 8/9 pattern: `awk` tracks fence-open/close state, flags any opening ``` fence line with an empty tag) run across the same C1 file set (excluding `tools/fixtures/` from the default sweep); zero untagged fence openers allowed.
- C4 (NIZAM.json index integrity): `NIZAM.json` parses as JSON and validates via `python3` + `jsonschema` against `registry/nizam-index.schema.json`; AND every repository-relative path indexed anywhere in `NIZAM.json` resolves on disk -- reuse the generalised walker logic proven in contract 006's verification (walks every string value under a `path` or `authoritative_source` key, plus every string item of any `key_documents`/`schemas`/`templates` list, skipping the literal `NA`) so this check is symmetric with the walker's own regression coverage.
- C5 (branding/endpoint leakage): zero occurrences of the case-insensitive strings `nizamiq`, `.svc`, `cluster.local` anywhere in shipped content (the C1 file set plus `NIZAM.json`, `CHANGELOG.md`, root `README.md`, and `bootstrap.sh`), using a guarded grep that first confirms the searched file set is non-empty before trusting an absence-of-match result (never a bare vacuous `! grep`).
- C6 (bootstrap.sh sanity): `bash -n ./bootstrap.sh` (syntax check) AND `timeout 5 ./bootstrap.sh --help` exits 0 (help mode is instant and network-free).
- C7 (module README presence, NDS Sec 5.3): each of `standard`, `methodology`, `registry`, `templates`, `schema`, `tools` contains a `README.md`.
- C8 (NDS Sec 4 version-bump-vs-changelog): for each file in the C1 set, read its current frontmatter `version`; compare against the same file's frontmatter `version` at `git show HEAD:<path>` (parsed the same way); if the file did not exist at `HEAD` (new file), skip the diff comparison for it (no prior version to compare against, no change record required by this check); if versions differ, require either (a) a `change_log` frontmatter entry on the file whose `version` matches the new version, or (b) a line in root `CHANGELOG.md` naming the file's path -- if neither is present, FAIL naming the file and the version delta.

Every check function is independently unit-testable in isolation via `--target` where the check applies to a single file (C1/C2/C3 for `.md` targets, C4 for a `.json` target shaped like an index file). Checks that are inherently repo-wide only (C5 leakage sweep across the whole set, C6 bootstrap.sh, C7 module READMEs, C8 git-history diff) do not run under `--target` (documented as such in `--help`); `--target` mode's exit code reflects only the checks applicable to the named file.

Intentional NEGATIVE fixture exercising C1/C2 (format-not-presence). Begins with a header comment (an HTML comment `<!-- INTENTIONAL NEGATIVE FIXTURE ... -->` immediately as the very first line, followed by valid 6-key frontmatter) -- NOTE: because NDS Sec 5.1 requires frontmatter to be the first content in a governed file with nothing preceding it, and this fixture is NEVER part of the default sweep (only reachable via `--target`, which is explicitly exempt from that placement rule for fixture files since fixtures are never sweep-governed documents), the leading HTML comment is authorized here specifically as the negative-fixture marker. All 6 frontmatter keys present and individually well-formed (kebab-case id, non-empty title/description, semver version, valid status enum) EXCEPT `authoritative_source`, which is deliberately set to a value that does NOT equal this file's own repo-relative path (e.g. `standard/NDS.md`, a real path but the wrong one) -- so the file passes schema presence validation (C1) but fails the format check (C2). Running `bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/bad_authoritative_source.md` MUST exit non-zero.

Intentional NEGATIVE fixture exercising C3 (untagged fence sweep). Begins with a header comment (`<!-- INTENTIONAL NEGATIVE FIXTURE ... -->`) followed by fully valid 6-key frontmatter (including a correct, self-referential `authoritative_source: tools/fixtures/untagged_fence.md`) so C1/C2 both PASS on this file in isolation -- it isolates C3 as the sole failing check. Body contains at least one fenced code block opened with a bare ``` (no language tag) per NDS Sec 6.2. Running `bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/untagged_fence.md` MUST exit non-zero.

Intentional NEGATIVE fixture exercising C4 (NIZAM.json schema validation + path walker), reachable only via `--target`. A JSON document shaped to satisfy `registry/nizam-index.schema.json`'s structural requirements (a minimal but schema-valid `framework`/`modules`/`capabilities`/`schemas`/`templates`/`consumption_guidance`/`index_schema`/`self_reference` object) so schema validation passes, but containing at least one `authoritative_source` or `path` value that points to a file that does NOT exist on disk (e.g. `tools/fixtures/does-not-exist.md`) -- isolating the path-walker failure as the sole cause of the non-zero exit, not a schema-shape failure. Since JSON has no native comment syntax, the intentional-negative-fixture marker is a top-level `_fixture_notice` string key (e.g. `"_fixture_notice": "INTENTIONAL NEGATIVE FIXTURE - broken indexed path, used only via tools/validate.sh --target"`), which `additionalProperties: true` on the schema's root object permits without breaking schema validation. Running `bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/broken_index.json` MUST exit non-zero.

files_modify.purpose: Two coordinated edits, no others: (1) append one new entry to the `capabilities` array (growing it from 13 to 14 entries) with `id: "nizam-compliance-validator"`, a `summary` describing the repo-local NDS Sec 7 enforcement validator (frontmatter+format+fence+index+branding+bootstrap+README+version-changelog checks), and `authoritative_source: "tools/validate.sh"`. (2) append `"tools/validate.sh"` to the existing `tools` module's `key_documents` array (the module entry whose `path` is `"tools"`), preserving the four existing entries (`tools/skill.json`, `tools/SKILL.md`, `tools/interface.md`, `tools/README.md`) byte-for-byte and their order. No other module, capability, schema, template, or top-level key changes. The full file MUST remain valid JSON and continue to validate against `registry/nizam-index.schema.json` after the edit, and every path it indexes (including the two newly added `tools/validate.sh` references) MUST resolve on disk.

non_goals: No .github/workflows/compliance.yml -- the CI workflow wiring belongs to feature 013
No edits to methodology/ or standard/ document content -- protocol hardening belongs to feature 014
No edit to CHANGELOG.md -- phase-close changelog entries belong to feature 015

No changes to schema/ content (schema/frontmatter.schema.json, registry/nizam-index.schema.json, or any other schema/*.json file) -- tools/validate.sh reads and validates against the existing schemas, it does not modify them
No changes to standard/ content -- tools/validate.sh enforces standard/NDS.md, it does not amend it
No edit to docs/architecture/ADR-001-ci-compliance-enforcement.md -- that decision record is feature 011's completed deliverable
No edit to any file beyond tools/validate.sh, tools/fixtures/bad_authoritative_source.md, tools/fixtures/untagged_fence.md, tools/fixtures/broken_index.json, NIZAM.json, and this contract file

tools/fixtures/ is never included in the default (no-args) repo-wide sweep -- the three fixtures are reachable only via --target, so their intentional defects never cause the clean-tree default-mode run to fail

No installation or vendoring of python3, jsonschema, or PyYAML -- the validator assumes these are already present in the execution environment and fails closed with a named diagnostic if they are not, it does not attempt to install them

verification.acceptance_test: Validator exists and passes syntax: test -f tools/validate.sh && bash -n tools/validate.sh
verification.command: f=tools/validate.sh; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; bash -n "$f" && echo PASS || { echo "syntax error in $f"; exit 1; }
f=tools/validate.sh; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; test -x "$f" && echo PASS || { echo "$f is not executable"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: Validator exits 0 on the clean repo tree AND actually performed all 8 contracted checks (not a no-op stub): bash tools/validate.sh; test $? -eq 0; stdout contains [C1] through [C8] PASS lines and a SUMMARY: 8 passed, 0 failed final line
verification.command: f=tools/validate.sh; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; out=$(bash "$f" 2>&1); rc=$?; test "$rc" -eq 0 || { echo "validator exited $rc on the clean tree, expected 0"; echo "$out"; exit 1; }; missing=""; for n in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8; do echo "$out" | grep -Eq "^\[C${n}\] (PASS|FAIL)" || missing="$missing C$n"; done; test -z "$missing" || { echo "missing per-check tag(s):$missing -- stub or incomplete validator"; echo "$out"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -Eq '^SUMMARY: 8 passed, 0 failed$' || { echo "no 'SUMMARY: 8 passed, 0 failed' final line found"; echo "$out"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: NEGATIVE bad-value fixture drives non-zero exit: bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/bad_authoritative_source.md; test $? -ne 0
verification.command: v=tools/validate.sh; fx=tools/fixtures/bad_authoritative_source.md; test -f "$v" || { echo "MISSING $v"; exit 1; }; test -f "$fx" || { echo "MISSING $fx"; exit 1; }; bash "$v" --target "$fx"; rc=$?; test "$rc" -ne 0 && echo PASS || { echo "expected non-zero exit for $fx, got $rc"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: NEGATIVE untagged-fence fixture drives non-zero exit: bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/untagged_fence.md; test $? -ne 0
verification.command: v=tools/validate.sh; fx=tools/fixtures/untagged_fence.md; test -f "$v" || { echo "MISSING $v"; exit 1; }; test -f "$fx" || { echo "MISSING $fx"; exit 1; }; bash "$v" --target "$fx"; rc=$?; test "$rc" -ne 0 && echo PASS || { echo "expected non-zero exit for $fx, got $rc"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: NEGATIVE broken-indexed-path fixture drives non-zero exit: bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/broken_index.json; test $? -ne 0
verification.command: v=tools/validate.sh; fx=tools/fixtures/broken_index.json; test -f "$v" || { echo "MISSING $v"; exit 1; }; test -f "$fx" || { echo "MISSING $fx"; exit 1; }; bash "$v" --target "$fx"; rc=$?; test "$rc" -ne 0 && echo PASS || { echo "expected non-zero exit for $fx, got $rc"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: Validator performs FORMAT (not presence) checks: grep -q 'authoritative_source' tools/validate.sh && grep -Eq 'version|semver' tools/validate.sh
verification.command: f=tools/validate.sh; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; grep -q 'authoritative_source' "$f" || { echo "no authoritative_source reference found in $f"; exit 1; }; grep -Eq 'version|semver' "$f" || { echo "no version/semver reference found in $f"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: tools/validate.sh is indexed in NIZAM.json: grep -q 'tools/validate.sh' NIZAM.json
verification.command: test -f NIZAM.json || { echo 'MISSING NIZAM.json'; exit 1; }; grep -q 'tools/validate.sh' NIZAM.json && echo PASS || { echo 'tools/validate.sh not referenced in NIZAM.json'; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: NIZAM.json still validates against its schema: python3 -c 'import json,jsonschema; jsonschema.validate(json.load(open("NIZAM.json")), json.load(open("registry/nizam-index.schema.json")))'
verification.command:
test -f NIZAM.json || { echo 'MISSING NIZAM.json'; exit 1; }; test -f registry/nizam-index.schema.json || { echo 'MISSING registry/nizam-index.schema.json'; exit 1; }; python3 -c "
import json, jsonschema
schema = json.load(open('registry/nizam-index.schema.json'))
instance = json.load(open('NIZAM.json'))
jsonschema.validate(instance=instance, schema=schema)
assert len(instance.get('capabilities', [])) == 14, f\"capabilities count {len(instance.get('capabilities', []))} != 14\"
print('PASS')
"

verification.acceptance_test: Every path indexed in NIZAM.json resolves on disk (walker script iterates modules/capabilities/schemas/templates authoritative_source and path entries; each test -e succeeds)
Validator references its frontmatter validation target schema: grep -q 'schema/frontmatter.schema.json' tools/validate.sh
verification.command: f=tools/validate.sh; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; grep -q 'schema/frontmatter.schema.json' "$f" && echo PASS || { echo 'no reference to schema/frontmatter.schema.json found' ; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: robustness: the default (no-args) sweep excludes tools/fixtures/ -- validator exits 0 on the repo tree WITH the three defective fixtures present on disk, actually performing all 8 checks (SUMMARY: 8 passed, 0 failed), and the script itself structurally excludes the fixtures directory from its sweep (not merely an accidental pass)
verification.command: v=tools/validate.sh; test -f "$v" || { echo "MISSING $v"; exit 1; }; for fx in tools/fixtures/bad_authoritative_source.md tools/fixtures/untagged_fence.md tools/fixtures/broken_index.json; do test -f "$fx" || { echo "MISSING $fx (precondition: fixtures must be present for this check to be non-vacuous)"; exit 1; }; done; grep -q 'fixtures' "$v" || { echo "validator does not reference 'fixtures' anywhere -- cannot confirm structural exclusion"; exit 1; }; out=$(bash "$v" 2>&1); rc=$?; test "$rc" -eq 0 || { echo "validator exited $rc with fixtures present, expected 0"; echo "$out"; exit 1; }; missing=""; for n in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8; do echo "$out" | grep -Eq "^\[C${n}\] (PASS|FAIL)" || missing="$missing C$n"; done; test -z "$missing" || { echo "missing per-check tag(s):$missing -- stub or incomplete validator"; echo "$out"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -Eq '^SUMMARY: 8 passed, 0 failed$' || { echo "no 'SUMMARY: 8 passed, 0 failed' final line found with fixtures present"; echo "$out"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: robustness: tools/ contains exactly the expected file set -- README.md, SKILL.md, interface.md, skill.json, validate.sh, and a fixtures/ subdirectory holding exactly the 3 contracted negative fixtures, no stray files
verification.command: test -d tools || { echo 'MISSING tools directory'; exit 1; }; actual="$(LC_ALL=C find tools -maxdepth 1 -type f | LC_ALL=C sort | tr '\n' ' ')"; expected="tools/README.md tools/SKILL.md tools/interface.md tools/skill.json tools/validate.sh "; test "$actual" = "$expected" || { echo "unexpected tools/ top-level file set: got [$actual] expected [$expected]"; exit 1; }; test -d tools/fixtures || { echo 'MISSING tools/fixtures directory'; exit 1; }; fxactual="$(LC_ALL=C find tools/fixtures -maxdepth 1 -type f | LC_ALL=C sort | tr '\n' ' ')"; fxexpected="tools/fixtures/bad_authoritative_source.md tools/fixtures/broken_index.json tools/fixtures/untagged_fence.md "; test "$fxactual" = "$fxexpected" && echo PASS || { echo "unexpected tools/fixtures/ file set: got [$fxactual] expected [$fxexpected]"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: behavioral coverage: C5 (branding/endpoint leakage) actually fires -- on a working-tree-preserving copy (retaining uncommitted files, since this feature's own deliverables are uncommitted when this runs) with the literal string 'nizamiq' injected into a shipped .md, the validator exits non-zero and stdout contains an explicit [C5] FAIL line
verification.command: v=tools/validate.sh; test -f "$v" || { echo "MISSING $v"; exit 1; }; scratch=$(mktemp -d) || { echo 'mktemp failed'; exit 1; }; trap 'rm -rf "$scratch"' EXIT; cp -a . "$scratch/repo" || { echo 'cp -a failed'; exit 1; }; test -f "$scratch/repo/tools/validate.sh" || { echo 'MISSING tools/validate.sh in copy'; exit 1; }; test -f "$scratch/repo/CONTEXT.md" || { echo 'MISSING CONTEXT.md in copy'; exit 1; }; printf '\nnizamiq\n' >> "$scratch/repo/CONTEXT.md"; out=$(cd "$scratch/repo" && bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; test "$rc" -ne 0 || { echo "expected non-zero exit after nizamiq injection, got 0"; echo "$out"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -Eq '^\[C5\] FAIL' && echo PASS || { echo 'no [C5] FAIL line found'; echo "$out"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: behavioral coverage: C6 (bootstrap.sh sanity) actually fires -- on a working-tree-preserving copy (retaining uncommitted files, since this feature's own deliverables are uncommitted when this runs) with bootstrap.sh's syntax deliberately broken, the validator exits non-zero and stdout contains an explicit [C6] FAIL line
verification.command: v=tools/validate.sh; test -f "$v" || { echo "MISSING $v"; exit 1; }; scratch=$(mktemp -d) || { echo 'mktemp failed'; exit 1; }; trap 'rm -rf "$scratch"' EXIT; cp -a . "$scratch/repo" || { echo 'cp -a failed'; exit 1; }; test -f "$scratch/repo/tools/validate.sh" || { echo 'MISSING tools/validate.sh in copy'; exit 1; }; test -f "$scratch/repo/bootstrap.sh" || { echo 'MISSING bootstrap.sh in copy'; exit 1; }; printf '\nif [\n' >> "$scratch/repo/bootstrap.sh"; bash -n "$scratch/repo/bootstrap.sh" 2>/dev/null && { echo 'mutation did not break bootstrap.sh syntax as expected'; exit 1; }; out=$(cd "$scratch/repo" && bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; test "$rc" -ne 0 || { echo "expected non-zero exit after corrupting bootstrap.sh, got 0"; echo "$out"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -Eq '^\[C6\] FAIL' && echo PASS || { echo 'no [C6] FAIL line found'; echo "$out"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: behavioral coverage: C7 (module README presence) actually fires -- on a working-tree-preserving copy (retaining uncommitted files, since this feature's own deliverables are uncommitted when this runs) with registry/README.md deleted, the validator exits non-zero and stdout contains an explicit [C7] FAIL line
verification.command: v=tools/validate.sh; test -f "$v" || { echo "MISSING $v"; exit 1; }; scratch=$(mktemp -d) || { echo 'mktemp failed'; exit 1; }; trap 'rm -rf "$scratch"' EXIT; cp -a . "$scratch/repo" || { echo 'cp -a failed'; exit 1; }; test -f "$scratch/repo/tools/validate.sh" || { echo 'MISSING tools/validate.sh in copy'; exit 1; }; test -f "$scratch/repo/registry/README.md" || { echo 'MISSING registry/README.md in copy (precondition)'; exit 1; }; rm -f "$scratch/repo/registry/README.md"; out=$(cd "$scratch/repo" && bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; test "$rc" -ne 0 || { echo "expected non-zero exit after deleting registry/README.md, got 0"; echo "$out"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -Eq '^\[C7\] FAIL' && echo PASS || { echo 'no [C7] FAIL line found'; echo "$out"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: behavioral coverage: C8 (version-bump-vs-changelog) actually fires -- on a working-tree-preserving copy that retains the .git directory (so HEAD comparison is meaningful; retaining uncommitted files too, since this feature's own deliverables are uncommitted when this runs) with a shipped doc's frontmatter version bumped and NO corresponding change_log entry or CHANGELOG.md line added, the validator exits non-zero and stdout contains an explicit [C8] FAIL line
verification.command: v=tools/validate.sh; test -f "$v" || { echo "MISSING $v"; exit 1; }; scratch=$(mktemp -d) || { echo 'mktemp failed'; exit 1; }; trap 'rm -rf "$scratch"' EXIT; cp -a . "$scratch/repo" || { echo 'cp -a failed'; exit 1; }; test -d "$scratch/repo/.git" || { echo 'MISSING .git in copy -- HEAD comparison would not be meaningful'; exit 1; }; test -f "$scratch/repo/tools/validate.sh" || { echo 'MISSING tools/validate.sh in copy'; exit 1; }; f="$scratch/repo/CONTEXT.md"; test -f "$f" || { echo 'MISSING CONTEXT.md in copy'; exit 1; }; grep -q '^version: 0.1.0$' "$f" || { echo 'expected version line "version: 0.1.0" not found in copied CONTEXT.md, cannot safely mutate'; exit 1; }; sed -i 's/^version: 0.1.0$/version: 0.2.0/' "$f"; grep -q '^version: 0.2.0$' "$f" || { echo 'version mutation did not apply'; exit 1; }; grep -q 'change_log' "$f" && { echo 'unexpected pre-existing change_log key would confound this test'; exit 1; }; out=$(cd "$scratch/repo" && bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; test "$rc" -ne 0 || { echo "expected non-zero exit after unrecorded version bump, got 0"; echo "$out"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -Eq '^\[C8\] FAIL' && echo PASS || { echo 'no [C8] FAIL line found'; echo "$out"; exit 1; }

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/012-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (e.g. .agent/evidence/012-verify-01.txt for the first entry, ..., .agent/evidence/012-verify-17.txt for the seventeenth). One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.
